response:
# Account Registration Form Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, cognitive accessibility users > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only navigation **Feature:** Multi-section financial services account registration form with static help text, conditional business fields, expandable Tax ID help, inline validation, and dependent phone fields. **Risk Level:** Medium-High **Component/Page Type:** Multi-section form with conditional fields, validation, disclosure help, and disabled dependent field Target document path if written later: `docs/a11y-plans/2026-06-12-account-registration-form-a11y-plan.md` --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers a new React/Formik account registration form for a financial services platform. The core accessibility risk is not whether fields have labels; it is whether screen reader users receive enough instruction at the right moment without confusing help text, error text, conditional content, and disabled dependent fields. The main ambiguity is help text timing. For this form, static field help text should be associated directly with each input using `aria-describedby`, so it is announced on field focus after the field label and role. This is the right tradeoff because the help text contains actionable requirements, such as password rules and financial identity guidance. In a financial services registration flow, missing instructions can cause account creation failure, lockout, or submission errors. Verbosity is a real concern, but it is less harmful here than silent requirements. What this plan is not claiming: this does not mean every minor hint in every form should always be announced on focus. Decorative or nonessential marketing copy should not be linked. This decision applies to required, actionable, field-level instructions needed to complete the form correctly. Primary WCAG grounding: - WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships: labels, help text, errors, grouped fields, and conditional sections must be programmatically related. - WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard: all fields, buttons, radio groups, and checkboxes must work by keyboard. - WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order: reveal/hide behavior must preserve logical focus order. - WCAG 2.4.6 Headings and Labels: labels and group legends must describe purpose. - WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification: validation errors must be clearly identified. - WCAG 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions: required formats and input guidance must be provided. - WCAG 3.3.7 Redundant Entry: previously entered phone data should be reused for the “same as primary phone” dependency. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value: controls must expose names, roles, and states. - WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages: conditional reveal and form-level status updates must be announced without stealing focus unnecessarily. ## Semantic Structure Plan Use native form semantics wherever possible. React/Formik should manage state, but the rendered DOM must remain plain, predictable form markup. ### Page and Form Structure - The registration page uses a single `main` landmark with one `h1`: “Create your account.” - The form uses a native `form` element. - Major sections use `fieldset` and `legend` where fields are conceptually grouped: - Personal information - Account type - Business details, conditionally rendered - Contact information - Security - Each input has a visible `label` using `for`/`id`. - Each static help text block has a stable `id`. - Each error message has a stable `id`. - Required fields should be indicated with visible text, not color alone, such as “Name required” or a visible “Required” marker explained once near the form start. ### Structure Diagram ```html

Create your account

All fields marked required must be completed.

Personal information
Account type
Business details
Contact information
Security
``` Semantic decisions: - Use `fieldset`/`legend` for radio groups and related conditional business fields: WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6. - Use native inputs, radios, checkboxes, and buttons instead of custom widgets: WCAG 4.1.2. - Use `display: none` or conditional rendering for hidden business fields. This correctly removes hidden fields from the accessibility tree and keyboard order. Do not add `aria-hidden="true"` on top of `display: none`; it is redundant and can create maintenance risk. ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Text inputs: Name, Email, Password, Company Name, Tax ID, Primary Phone, Mobile Phone | Native text input pattern | Tab moves between fields; typing edits; Shift+Tab reverses | `aria-describedby` for help/error IDs; `aria-invalid` when invalid | 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 | | Account type radio group | APG Radio Group: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/radio/ | Tab enters group; Arrow keys move between options; Space selects focused option | Native `fieldset`/`legend`; native radio checked state | 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 | | “What is a Tax ID?” help button | APG Disclosure: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ | Tab focuses button; Enter/Space toggles; focus remains on button | `aria-expanded`; `aria-controls` | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | “Same as primary phone” checkbox | Native checkbox pattern | Tab focuses checkbox; Space toggles | Native checked state; `aria-describedby` if explanatory text exists | 2.1.1, 3.3.7, 4.1.2 | | Submit button | Native button pattern | Tab focuses; Enter/Space activates | Native button name and disabled state if needed | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | ### Help Text Association Decision Static, actionable help text should be connected to each input with `aria-describedby`. Example structure stub: ```html

Must be 8 or more characters and include one number.

``` Screen reader announcement order should be: 1. Label: “Password” 2. Role/type: “edit protected” or equivalent 3. State if present: “invalid” when applicable 4. Description: “Must be 8 or more characters and include one number.” Reasoning: - Field-level help contains completion-critical instructions. - Announcing it on focus prevents blind or low-vision users from discovering requirements only after failure. - WCAG 3.3.2 requires labels or instructions when input expectations exist. - WCAG 1.3.1 requires relationships to be programmatically determinable. ### Error Plus Help Announcement Order When both an error and help text exist, include both IDs in `aria-describedby`, with the error ID first and help ID second. ```html

Error: Email is already in use.

Use the email address you want associated with this account.

``` Expected announcement: “Email, edit, invalid. Error: Email is already in use. Use the email address you want associated with this account.” This ordering matters. Error first supports immediate recovery; help second preserves guidance. This satisfies WCAG 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 1.3.1, and 4.1.2. ## Focus Management Plan Default focus should remain stable unless the user performs an action that clearly changes the task context. ### Tab Order Recommended order: 1. Skip link, if global navigation exists 2. Name 3. Email 4. Account type radio group 5. Company Name, only when Business Account is selected 6. Tax ID, only when Business Account is selected 7. “What is a Tax ID?” disclosure button, only when Tax ID is visible 8. Primary Phone 9. Same as primary phone checkbox 10. Mobile Phone, unless disabled 11. Password 12. Submit button ### Conditional Business Fields When the user selects “Business Account,” reveal the Business details fieldset using conditional rendering or `display: block`. Hidden state should be `display: none` or unmounted. Chosen announcement strategy: use a single polite status region near the account type group, not automatic focus movement. When “Business Account” is selected, update the status region with: “Business details fields are now available: Company Name and Tax ID.” When “Personal Account” is selected, update it with: “Business details fields hidden.” Justification: - The user is still interacting with the account type radio group. Moving focus automatically to Company Name could be disorienting and interrupt radio navigation. - A polite status message announces the change without stealing focus. - The newly revealed fields appear next in logical tab order, so keyboard users naturally encounter them. - WCAG 4.1.3 supports status announcements without focus change; WCAG 2.4.3 supports preserving predictable focus order. What this is not claiming: focus movement is never valid. In a wizard-like flow where the user explicitly clicks “Continue to business details,” moving focus to the next section heading or first field may be better. For this inline radio-driven reveal, polite status is the better default. ### Expandable Tax ID Help The Tax ID help button follows the APG Disclosure pattern. - Focus stays on the help button when expanded. - The inline help content appears below the Tax ID field/help button. - Do not use `aria-live` on the expandable help content. The user requested the expansion; `aria-expanded` communicates the state, and the content is available immediately after the button in reading order. - The button’s accessible name should be text, not just “?”. Use visible text if possible: “What is a Tax ID?” If the visual design insists on a “?” icon, the button must have `aria-label="What is a Tax ID?"`, and the icon must be `aria-hidden="true"`. Required attributes: ```html ``` The expandable region should have `id="tax-id-expanded-help"` and be hidden when collapsed with `hidden` or `display: none`. ### Disabled Mobile Phone Field When “Same as primary phone” is checked: - Populate Mobile Phone with Primary Phone value. - Use the native HTML `disabled` attribute on Mobile Phone. - Do not use `aria-disabled="true"` for this native input. Reasoning: - Native `disabled` removes the field from tab order, prevents editing, and exposes disabled state correctly to assistive technology. - `aria-disabled="true"` only communicates disabled state; it does not prevent focus or editing by itself. - Since the field is not editable while synced, removing it from tab order is appropriate. - WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WCAG 3.3.7 Redundant Entry. Add visible explanatory text near the checkbox or mobile field: “Mobile phone will use the primary phone number.” Associate that text with the checkbox using `aria-describedby` if it is not already obvious. ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA Attribute / HTML | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Help text available | Text below field | Description associated with input | `aria-describedby="field-help"` | 1.3.1, 3.3.2 | | Field invalid | Error text, icon, red border | Invalid state and error association | `aria-invalid="true"`, `aria-describedby="field-error field-help"` | 1.3.1, 3.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Field valid | No error text; normal border | Remove invalid state | Omit `aria-invalid` or set `false`; remove error ID from `aria-describedby` | 4.1.2 | | Business fields hidden | Fields not visible | Removed from accessibility tree and tab order | `display: none` or unmounted | 1.3.1, 2.4.3 | | Business fields revealed | Business details fieldset appears | Polite status message announces reveal | `role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` | 4.1.3 | | Tax ID help collapsed | Help content hidden; button indicates closed | Button exposes collapsed state | `aria-expanded="false"`, `aria-controls` | 4.1.2 | | Tax ID help expanded | Help content visible below field | Button exposes expanded state | `aria-expanded="true"`, `aria-controls` | 4.1.2 | | Same as primary checked | Checkbox visibly checked; mobile field disabled | Native checked and disabled states | `checked`, `disabled` | 3.3.7, 4.1.2 | | Mobile Phone disabled | Gray styling plus text explanation | Native disabled state | `disabled` | 4.1.2 | | Form submitting | Submit button disabled; status text “Creating account…” | Status region and busy state on form area | `aria-busy="true"` on form region; `role="status"` message | 4.1.3 | | Submit failure | Form-level error summary | Alert/status and links to invalid fields | `role="alert"` for submit failure summary; field-level `aria-invalid` | 3.3.1, 4.1.3 | Use one status region per event class. Do not put `role="alert"` on every repeated field error in a loop. Field-level errors should be connected with `aria-describedby`; form-level submit failures may use one `role="alert"` summary. ## Visual Accessibility Plan - Normal text must meet 4.5:1 contrast: WCAG 1.4.3. - Large text must meet 3:1 contrast: WCAG 1.4.3. - Form borders, focus indicators, icons, and disabled field affordances must meet 3:1 non-text contrast where they convey state: WCAG 1.4.11. - Error state must not be red alone. Use red border plus visible error text and an icon or “Error:” prefix: WCAG 1.4.1. - Required state must not be indicated only by an asterisk unless the asterisk is explained in text. - Focus indicators should use a two-color visible ring, at least 2 CSS pixels thick, with 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors: WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.13. - All controls should have at least 24x24 CSS pixel target size, with 44x44 recommended for icon buttons like the Tax ID help button: WCAG 2.5.8. - Text should use relative units and reflow at 200% and 400% zoom without horizontal scrolling: WCAG 1.4.4 and 1.4.10. - If the form uses transitions for expanding the Business details or Tax ID help, respect `prefers-reduced-motion`: WCAG 2.3.3. ## Content Accessibility Plan ### Labels Every field needs a visible label: - Name - Email - Password - Account type - Company Name - Tax ID - Primary Phone - Same as primary phone - Mobile Phone Do not rely on placeholder text as the only label. Placeholder text disappears and is not a robust accessible name. ### Help Text Help text should be concise, instructional, and associated with the input. Examples: - Password help: “Must be 8 or more characters and include one number.” - Email help: “Use the email address you want associated with this account.” - Tax ID static help, if any: “Required for business accounts.” The expanded Tax ID explanation is separate from ordinary field help. It is on-demand explanatory content controlled by the disclosure button. ### Error Text Errors must be specific and actionable: - Good: “Email is already in use. Use a different email address or sign in.” - Bad: “Invalid input.” Prefix visible error text with “Error:” so the meaning does not depend on color or icon. ### Icon Button Content The “?” icon must not be the accessible name. Preferred visible text: “What is a Tax ID?” Acceptable compact pattern: ```html ``` Do not use `title` as the accessible name. `title` is not reliable enough for buttons and does not satisfy this design requirement. ## Testing Strategy ### Automated Runtime Testing Run axe-core against these states: - Initial form, Personal Account selected - Business Account selected with Business details visible - Tax ID help expanded - Field errors visible after blur - Same as primary phone checked with Mobile Phone disabled - Submit failure with form-level error summary Check for: - Missing labels - Invalid ARIA attributes - Incorrect `aria-describedby` references - Color contrast failures - Landmark and heading issues - Form field grouping issues ### DOM Verification Required DOM checks: - Each input has a visible label connected with `for`/`id`. - Help IDs referenced in `aria-describedby` exist in the DOM. - When an error exists, `aria-describedby` lists error ID before help ID. - `aria-invalid="true"` appears only on invalid fields. - Business details are absent from the accessibility tree when hidden by `display: none` or unmounted. - Business details fieldset and legend exist when Business Account is selected. - Tax ID button has `aria-expanded` and `aria-controls`, and the controlled region ID matches. - Mobile Phone uses native `disabled`, not only `aria-disabled`. ### Keyboard Testing - Tab through the form in order. - Arrow through Account type radio buttons. - Select Business Account with keyboard and confirm status message is announced. - Continue tabbing and confirm Company Name and Tax ID appear next in order. - Focus Tax ID help button and press Space/Enter; confirm help expands and focus remains on button. - Press Space on Same as primary phone; confirm Mobile Phone is populated and removed from tab order. - Submit with invalid fields; confirm focus behavior follows the implementation rule below. For submit errors, recommended focus behavior: - On failed submit, move focus to the form-level error summary if one is shown. - The summary should link to each invalid field. - Activating an error link moves focus to the corresponding field. - Field-level errors remain associated through `aria-describedby`. ### Screen Reader Testing Test with NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver: - On Email focus, screen reader announces label, role, and help text. - On invalid Email focus, screen reader announces label, invalid state, error first, then help text. - On Password focus, password rules are announced. - Selecting Business Account announces “Business details fields are now available: Company Name and Tax ID.” - Hidden Business details are not reachable when Personal Account is selected. - Tax ID help button announces name, button role, and expanded/collapsed state. - Expanding Tax ID help does not cause duplicate live announcements. - Mobile Phone is skipped when disabled and same-as-primary is checked. ## Implementation Tasks ### Task 1: Form Semantics and Field Groups 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Files:** - Account registration form component - Field/input helper component, if used - Formik validation schema or validation module **Structure Stub:** ```html
Personal information
Account type
Contact information
Security
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-describedby` on form only for global form instructions: WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2. - `role="status"` on the conditional update region: WCAG 4.1.3. **Tests:** - Verify fieldsets and legends exist. - Verify radio group is grouped by fieldset/legend. - Verify form instructions are referenced correctly. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.4.6 Headings and Labels - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions ### Task 2: Field-Level Help and Error Association 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html

Error: Email is already in use.

Use the email address you want associated with this account.

``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-describedby`: help and error association; error ID first when present: WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.1, 3.3.2. - `aria-invalid`: invalid field state: WCAG 4.1.2. **Tests:** - Verify help text is announced on focus. - Verify error is announced before help. - Verify `aria-describedby` removes error ID when field becomes valid. - Verify error text is visible and specific. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 3.3.1 Error Identification - 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Task 3: Conditional Business Fields 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
Account type
Business details
``` **Behavior:** - Personal Account hides Business details with unmounting or `display: none`. - Business Account reveals Business details. - Update the polite status region on reveal/hide. - Do not move focus automatically. **Tests:** - Verify Business details are not tabbable when hidden. - Verify Business details are not in accessibility tree when hidden. - Verify polite status updates on Business Account selection. - Verify newly revealed fields follow next in tab order. **WCAG Criteria:** - 1.3.1 Info and Relationships - 2.4.3 Focus Order - 4.1.3 Status Messages ### Task 4: Tax ID Expandable Help 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html
A Tax ID is an Employer Identification Number used to identify a business for tax purposes.
``` **ARIA Attributes:** - `aria-expanded`: exposes open/closed state: WCAG 4.1.2. - `aria-controls`: connects button to controlled help region: WCAG 4.1.2. **Keyboard Interactions:** - Tab focuses button. - Enter/Space toggles help. - Focus remains on button. **Tests:** - Verify `aria-expanded` toggles. - Verify `aria-controls` points to the correct region. - Verify no `aria-live` is used on the expandable help. - Verify focus remains on button after expansion. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ### Task 5: Same as Primary Phone Dependency 🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task** **Structure Stub:** ```html

Mobile phone will use the primary phone number.

``` **Behavior:** - Checking the checkbox copies Primary Phone into Mobile Phone. - Mobile Phone becomes disabled using native `disabled`. - Unchecking re-enables Mobile Phone and allows editing. **Tests:** - Verify Space toggles checkbox. - Verify Mobile Phone receives native `disabled`. - Verify disabled Mobile Phone is skipped in tab order. - Verify value is populated from Primary Phone. - Verify no `aria-disabled` substitute is used. **WCAG Criteria:** - 2.1.1 Keyboard - 3.3.7 Redundant Entry - 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value ## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints | Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas | |---|---|---| | 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Verify fieldset/legend grouping, form instructions, semantic structure | | 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Verify help/error `aria-describedby` strategy, error-first ID order, `aria-invalid` correctness | | 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Verify conditional reveal announcement, hidden field removal, tab order | | 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Verify APG Disclosure pattern, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, no unnecessary live region | | 🔍 5 | Task 5 | Verify native disabled behavior, dependency explanation, keyboard behavior | ## Architecture Overview The form uses native HTML form controls with Formik state management. Accessibility depends on five core patterns: - Native input with visible label and `aria-describedby` - Native radio group grouped by `fieldset`/`legend` - APG Disclosure pattern for Tax ID expandable help - Polite status region for conditional Business details reveal/hide - Native checkbox plus native disabled input for phone dependency No custom ARIA widget should be introduced unless native controls cannot meet the requirement. ## Failure Modes This plan prevents these specific failures: - Help text visible on screen but not announced to screen reader users. - Error text and help text announced in confusing order. - Business fields visually hidden but still reachable by keyboard. - Business fields appearing silently after account type selection. - Tax ID “?” button announced only as “question mark button.” - Tax ID help treated as a live region and announced redundantly. - Mobile Phone marked with `aria-disabled` but still editable or focusable. - Business fields not grouped under a meaningful “Business details” legend. - Errors communicated only by red borders. - Form tests checking visual output but not rendered ARIA relationships. ## References - WAI-ARIA APG Radio Group Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/radio/ - WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure Pattern: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ - WCAG 2.2 Quick Reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/quickref/ - a11y-critic review should run after implementation and after keyboard/screen reader testing.
